task_completion.description: Open NASA Panoply and load the NCEP precipitation dataset.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Panoply GUI is open and 'prate.sfc.mon.ltm.nc' is visible in the Datasets tab.

task_completion.description: Create a geo-mapped plot for the month of April.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Panoply plot window showing a 2D map of precipitation ('prate'). The time/array dimension in the plot controls should be set to April (or index 4 out of 12).

task_completion.description: Export the geo-mapped plot to the specified directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager or terminal window showing the file 'precip_map_april.png' located in '~/Documents/EastAfricaRainfall/'.

task_completion.description: Create a 1D line plot of the 12-month annual cycle for the specified coordinates.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Panoply plot window showing a 1D line graph with time (12 months) on the x-axis and precipitation on the y-axis. The plot controls must show the latitude set near 0°N and longitude near 37.5°E.

task_completion.description: Export the 1D line plot to the specified directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager or terminal window showing the file 'annual_cycle_lineplot.png' located in '~/Documents/EastAfricaRainfall/'.

task_completion.description: Create a structured report identifying the bimodal rainfall pattern and peak months.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., gedit, nano, mousepad) showing 'rainfall_assessment.txt' with content mentioning a 'bimodal' pattern, and identifying the peak months (March, April, May for long rains; October, November, December for short rains).

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the NASA Panoply GUI to generate the plots.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show active interaction with the Panoply Java application interface (menus, plot settings, export dialogs), rather than using Python scripts or terminal commands to generate the plots.

integrity.description: Verify the exported plots match the Panoply visual style.
integrity.visual_evidence: The exported PNG files (if visible) or the plots in the Panoply window should have the distinct Panoply styling (specific color bars, title formatting, map projections) and not look like generic matplotlib outputs.

integrity.description: Verify the agent created the text report using a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: A text editor application should be visible on screen with the agent typing or formatting the rainfall assessment report, rather than just echoing text directly to a file blindly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The correct variable to plot is 'prate' (Precipitation Rate). The 1D line plot must be centered near latitude 0°N (acceptable range -5.0 to 5.0) and longitude 37.5°E (acceptable range 30.0 to 45.0). The rainfall assessment report must identify the pattern as 'BIMODAL', with 'Long Rains' peaking in March, April, May (MAM) and 'Short Rains' peaking in October, November, December (OND).
